chunks.text: On Monday morning, I get up at half past six.
In the kitchen I make coffee and open the newspaper.
At the station I take the train and commute to my office.
In a crowded car, many passengers stand silently.
I attend a small company in the local industry.
My coworkers are kind, and a colleague often laughs loudly.
Each person carries out his duty.
At noon we gather for lunch together.
In the afternoon, I finish one task after another.
The phone rings, and a new request appears.
Work proceeds in a steady way.
In the evening the same common faces return to the station, and people rush home.
Through the train window I look beyond the tall buildings at the fading sky.
Inside these quiet days, a small faith lives, and I believe it.
